raw: {"weights": {"R1.1": 0.3, "R1.2": 0.7, "R1.3": 0.3, "R1.4": 0.0, "R1.5": 0.3, "R1.6": 0.7, "R1.7": 0.3, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0}, "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"], "rationale": {"R1.2": "The artifact makes precise, falsifiable mathematical claims—convergence rate O(L_μ·ε^{-1/2}), strong-duality interchange via Slater's condition, closed-form proximal operator, Lipschitz modulus L_μ=ρ/μ—that must be internally consistent and correctly derived; verifying their logical coherence is central to any substantive technical review.", "R1.6": "\"Take a look\" without further qualification naturally encompasses an assessment of sentence clarity, exposition flow, and paragraph-level quality, making writing polish a centrally expected responsibility alongside technical checking."}, "clarification_needed": true, "clarification_question": "Should I prioritize technical/mathematical correctness—checking the convergence analysis, duality arguments, and proximal-operator derivations for logical errors—or writing clarity and polish—improving exposition, sentence quality, and narrative flow (e.g., the out-of-order presentation of convergence results before problem formulation)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes precise, falsifiable mathematical claims—convergence rate O(L_μ·ε^{-1/2}), strong-duality interchange via Slater's condition, closed-form proximal operator, Lipschitz modulus L_μ=ρ/μ—that must be internally consistent and correctly derived; verifying their logical coherence is central to any substantive technical review.
rationale.R1.6: "Take a look" without further qualification naturally encompasses an assessment of sentence clarity, exposition flow, and paragraph-level quality, making writing polish a centrally expected responsibility alongside technical checking.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I prioritize technical/mathematical correctness—checking the convergence analysis, duality arguments, and proximal-operator derivations for logical errors—or writing clarity and polish—improving exposition, sentence quality, and narrative flow (e.g., the out-of-order presentation of convergence results before problem formulation)?
